fix_patch:
diff --git a/AGENTS.md b/AGENTS.md
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..e9567c573
--- /dev/null
+++ b/AGENTS.md
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+# Hugo Repository Notes
+
+## Key Packages
+
+- **metrics/**: Template metrics tracking for `--templateMetrics` and `--templateMetricsHints` flags
+  - `diff.add()` tracks similarity of template inputs over time
+  - `howSimilar()` compares values; handles string types, compare.Eqer, and compare.ProbablyEqer
+  - `howSimilarStrings()` does word-based similarity with partition weighting
+
+## Known Pre-existing Test Failures
+
+These tests fail even without any code changes (environment issues):
+- `hugolib.TestHugoModulesTargetInSubFolder`
+- `hugolib.TestMountsPaths`
+- `hugolib.TestImageOps`
+- `hugolib.TestPageBundlerSiteWitSymbolicLinksInContent`
+- `hugolib.TestSiteWithGoModButNoModules`
+- `hugolib.TestMountsContentFile`
+- `hugolib.TestPageBundlerHome`
+- `hugolib.TestModuleAbsMount`
+- `tpl/internal/go_templates/texttemplate.TestJSEscaping`
+
+## Template Metrics Architecture
+
+Template metrics are collected in two ways:
+1. `MeasureSince()` - called from `tpl/tplimpl/template.go` for every template execution
+2. `TrackValue()` - called from `tpl/partials/partials.go` for partial template output values
+
+The `cacheFactor` = `floor(simSum / diffCount)` represents how cache-friendly a template is
+(how similar its input values are across executions). Higher = more cache-friendly.
diff --git a/metrics/metrics.go b/metrics/metrics.go
index 329981202..9fc7c76fa 100644
--- a/metrics/metrics.go
+++ b/metrics/metrics.go
@@ -18,6 +18,7 @@ import (
 	"fmt"
 	"io"
 	"math"
+	"reflect"
 	"sort"
 	"strconv"
 	"strings"
@@ -25,8 +26,6 @@ import (
 	"time"
 
 	"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/compare"
-
-	"github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hreflect"
 )
 
 // The Provider interface defines an interface for measuring metrics.
@@ -52,10 +51,10 @@ type diff struct {
 }
 
 func (d *diff) add(v interface{}) *diff {
-	if !hreflect.IsTruthful(v) {
+	if d.count == 0 {
 		d.baseline = v
 		d.count = 1
-		d.simSum = 100 // If we get only one it is very cache friendly.
+		d.simSum = 100 // First occurrence is trivially similar to itself.
 		return d
 	}
 
@@ -202,6 +201,18 @@ func howSimilar(a, b interface{}) int {
 	as, ok1 := a.(string)
 	bs, ok2 := b.(string)
 
+	if !ok1 {
+		// Handle string-like types, e.g. template.HTML.
+		if av := reflect.ValueOf(a); av.Kind() == reflect.String {
+			as, ok1 = av.String(), true
+		}
+	}
+	if !ok2 {
+		if bv := reflect.ValueOf(b); bv.Kind() == reflect.String {
+			bs, ok2 = bv.String(), true
+		}
+	}
+
 	if ok1 && ok2 {
 		return howSimilarStrings(as, bs)
 	}
@@ -238,6 +249,10 @@ func howSimilarStrings(a, b string) int {
 		af, bf = bf, af
 	}
 
+	if len(af) == 0 {
+		return 100
+	}
+
 	m1 := make(map[string]bool)
 	for i, x := range bf {
 		partition := partition(i, partitionSize)
